behavior_summary: Implements stair/tile relocation and type-change attempts for a single stair/item instance. The routines read an item's coordinates, orientation (Z), type and frame, then try to change the tile or move the item to an alternate position depending on orientation and type.

U8P_STAIR::func0192 is the higher-level attempt: it calls FREE::changeItemType with a requested type (param1) and on success clears a blocked flag; on failure it sets a blocked flag, plays an error SFX, and falls back to a recovery/local-move routine.

U8P_STAIR::func0080 is a lower-level/local recovery handler invoked when direct changes or moves fail; it computes an alternate new type or destination (based on Z masking and current type), grabs the item, and either calls FREE::changeItemType at an adjusted Z or checks Item::legal_move and spawns a continuation if legal.
Sound effects (playSFX) are used to signal action start, success, or failure; the global flag hanoiStairBlocked is used to mark when a stair-change operation is blocked after a failed attempt.
notable_control_flow: U8P_STAIR::func0192: attempt FREE::changeItemType(param1) -> if success: clear hanoiStairBlocked and play success SFX; suspend and re-evaluate -> branch by (Z & mask) to attempt alternate relocation or legal_move; on failure mark hanoiStairBlocked, play error SFX and call U8P_STAIR::func0080 as fallback.

U8P_STAIR::func0080: read X/Y/Z/type/frame, play initial SFX and suspend -> branch by (Z & 0x07) equality test to select either a type-to-type mapping and direct FREE::changeItemType at adjusted Z, or compute a destination coordinate and test Item::legal_move -> spawn continuation on success, otherwise treat as failure.
Both routines preserve/passthrough item frame when calling FREE::changeItemType and use Item::grab before attempting item modifications.
open_questions: Exact numeric mappings between source type ids and candidate replacement type ids (the small type->type table inferred from SetFlag patterns) — mapping is inferred but not concretely identified.
Precise semantic meaning of the Z bitmask tests ((local3 & 0x07) == 0x00 vs 0x06) — likely indicates vertical orientation (up/down) but the exact convention is inferred.
Broader usage and lifecycle of the global hanoiStairBlocked flag elsewhere in the game (how long it remains set and what systems observe it) — effect inferred from writes only.
Whether FREE::changeItemType returns strict boolean success/failure or richer error codes that are being interpreted as truthy/falsy here.

main_function.name: U8P_STAIR::func0080
main_function.purpose: Perform a local recovery or alternate stair relocation/type-change for the current item when a primary change/move failed: compute alternate type or destination based on item orientation and type, grab the item, attempt engine-level change or legal_move, and spawn follow-up processing on success.
entry_conditions: Invoked as a fallback/recovery from U8P_STAIR::func0192 after a failed change/move attempt.
Likely invoked directly when a stair/item needs local relocation or type adjustment (inferred from Item::grab usage and suspend points).

function_map.name: U8P_STAIR::func0080
function_map.role: local recovery / alternate stair relocation and type-change handler

function_map.name: U8P_STAIR::func0192
function_map.role: primary stair-change attempt routine that drives FREE::changeItemType(param1) and coordinates fallback logic

internal_calls.purpose: Fallback/local recovery when primary change/move fails (invoke alternate local relocation logic).

Preserve item frame/subtype when calling FREE::changeItemType.
Prepare/grab the item for modification before attempting change or move.

Check whether moving the item to a computed destination coordinate is permitted before spawning follow-up processing.
Spawn a continuation/follow-up process after a successful Item::legal_move.
Play action/success/failure sound effects to signal operation progress or errors.

writes.name: hanoiStairBlocked
writes.likely_effect: Marks whether a stair-change/move operation is blocked: set (0x01) on failure, cleared (0x00) on success. Used to prevent repeated/invalid attempts or signal error state to other systems.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements stair/tile relocation and type-change attempts for a single stair/item instance. The routines read an item's coordinates, orientation (Z), type and frame, then try to change the tile or move the item to an alternate position depending on orientation and type.
